title: TE Connectivity projects upbeat quarterly results, to buy Astrodyne for $1.4 billion
excerpt: Electronic equipment maker TE Connectivity on Wednesday forecast fourth-quarter profit and revenue above Wall Street expectations, betting on ‌a boom in dema...
content_html:
Anshuman Tripathy

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 6:13 AM EDT 2 min read

By Anshuman Tripathy

July 22 (Reuters) - Electronic equipment maker TE Connectivity on Wednesday forecast fourth-quarter profit and revenue above Wall Street expectations, betting on ‌a boom in demand for its AI-related tools and products.

Growing ‌demand for AI-related tools and products has boosted investments in data centers and network equipment globally, ​benefiting companies like TE, which produces electrical connector systems used in data centers.

CEO Terrence Curtin told Reuters in an interview that the company would stick to its strategy of passing on higher raw material costs through price hikes to ‌protect its margins, as ⁠the cost of oil-based products such as resins remains elevated amid renewed fighting between the U.S. and Iran.

When asked about ⁠refunds from President Donald Trump's tariffs, Curtin said the company had applied for them and had not "gotten big money back," adding that it would return the refunds ​to ​customers, rather than offering broad price cuts.

Here ​are some more details:

• TE ‌also said it would buy power management and filtering solutions maker Astrodyne TDI for $1.4 billion from private equity firm Tinicum LP.

• Shares of TE, which expects the deal to close by the end of 2026, were down about 7%.

• "Our orders are up 70% this year, building strong backlog into next ‌year," Curtin said.

• The company forecast an ​adjusted profit of $3.05 per share for the current ​quarter, compared with analysts' expectations ​of $2.96 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

• It ‌expects fourth-quarter revenue to be about $5.25 ​billion, above analysts' ​estimate of $5.16 billion.

• Revenue for the third quarter grew 14% to $5.16 billion, driven by growth in its industrial and transportation segments.

• Analysts on ​average had expected revenue ‌of $5 billion.

• TE reported an adjusted profit of $2.94 per share for ​the quarter ended June 26, beating estimates of $2.84.

(Reporting by Anshuman Tripathy ​in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

content_html_zh:
安舒曼三重病

2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三，美国东部时间上午 6:13 阅读 2 分钟

作者：安舒曼·特里帕蒂

7 月 22 日（路透社）——电子设备制造商 TE Con​​nectivity 周三预测第四季度利润和收入将高于华尔街预期，押注其人工智能相关工具和产品的需求将蓬勃发展。

对人工智能相关工具和产品的需求不断增长，推动了全球数据中心和网络设备的投资，使 TE 等生产数据中心使用的电气连接器系统的公司受益。

首席执行官特伦斯·科廷(Terrence Curtin)在接受路透社采访时表示，该公司将坚持通过提价转嫁原材料成本上涨的战略，以保护其利润率，因为在美国和伊朗之间的新一轮战斗中，树脂等石油基产品的成本仍然居高不下。

当被问及唐纳德·特朗普总统关税退款的问题时，科廷表示，该公司已申请退款，但尚未“收回大笔资金”，并补充说，它将把退款退还给客户，而不是提供广泛的降价。

以下是更多详细信息：

• TE 还表示，将以 14 亿美元的价格从私募股权公司 Tinicum LP 手中收购电源管理和滤波解决方案制造商 Astrodyne TDI。

• TE 预计该交易将于 2026 年底完成，该公司股价下跌约 7%。

• “我们今年的订单增长了 70%，为明年积压了大量订单，”Curtin 说。

• 根据 LSEG 汇编的数据，该公司预计本季度调整后每股利润为 3.05 美元，而分析师的预期为每股 2.96 美元。

• 预计第四季度收入约为 52.5 亿美元，高于分析师预期的 51.6 亿美元。

• 在工业和运输部门增长的推动下，第三季度收入增长14%，达到51.6 亿美元。

• 分析师平均预期收入为 50 亿美元。

• TE 报告截至 6 月 26 日的季度调整后每股利润为 2.94 美元，超出预期的 2.84 美元。

（班加罗尔 Ans human Tripathy 报道；Maju Samuel 编辑）
